summary: Audit every running timesheet approval chain, hide stale candidates from the chain view, and show real approval timestamps per project block.

notes: Repairs six legacy PMCC-template nodes that should have been skipped for consulting projects.
Removes an active stale unresolved candidate and lets the existing serial activation logic continue the affected chain.
Preserves cancelled assignee rows as audit history.
Adds a reusable running-timesheet chain integrity assertion covering pending-assignee cardinality, serial order, real approval actions, applicability, and current route resolution.
Approval-chain project rows show only effective or actual approvers; cancelled and delegated candidates remain in approval records only.
Approved project rows show the real acted_at timestamp beside the project name.
The current-stage footer is removed and the active stage is indicated with the Xinpin brand-red border and focus ring.

verification: Audit all 73 submitted/running timesheets and 847 runtime approval nodes on production.
Run npx vite build from frontend with production VITE_* variables and VITE_APP_VERSION=V0.18.40.
Dry-run migration 137 and scripts/assert-running-timesheet-approval-chain-integrity.sql in a production transaction, then roll back.
Run deploy/scripts/pre-deploy-check.sh on the cloud host with EXPECTED_VERSION=V0.18.40.
Run a production SPA root-render smoke check and verify V0.18.40 is visible.
known_warnings: PMCC26008, PMCC26012, and PMCC26013 currently lack configured PM cost owners for 19 future waiting nodes; no approver is fabricated, and the configured missing-assignee policy will skip those nodes only when reached.
